prompt: Explain when a MOO command verb should check player against this.owner instead of caller_perms().
expected_properties: states command UI checks often validate the connected player
uses this.owner as an owner policy example
does not claim player is the right authority for every programmatic helper

prompt: Write a programmatic MOO helper that changes this.setting only when the caller's effective permissions control this object.
expected_properties: uses a permission helper such as $perm_utils:controls(caller_perms(), this)
returns or raises E_PERM on failure
does not check player.wizard or player == this.owner as the main authority

prompt: Write a private internal helper that only another verb on this same object may call.
expected_properties: raises or returns E_PERM for outside callers
does not use caller_perms() as a substitute for call-path privacy
documents that caller is the invoking verb object

prompt: Explain why valid(object) is not always enough on LambdaCore-style cores that use $recycler and $garbage objects.
expected_properties: mentions $recycler as a common LambdaCore convention
states valid(object) can be true for soft-recycled garbage objects
recommends $recycler:valid(object) when the target core uses the recycler
distinguishes core convention from server-level recycling
